title: Edinburgh Comedy Preview
description:
Rosalie Minnit and Joseph Morpurgo preview their work-in-progress comedy shows ahead of the Edinburgh Fringe.

Get a sneak preview of some of the comedy shows that are about to make a splash at this year’s Edinburgh Fringe.

Rosalie Minnit

With neither sense nor sensibility, history’s most helpless heroine is back by popular(ish) demand. And this time, dear reader, she’s more unhinged than ever.

Delusional. Illiterate. And still a single sack of sh*t (as her Mama lovingly puts it.)

From the very last brain cell of award-winning comedian (Funny Women 2024) Rosalie Minnitt, comes a feral new fever dream for the girls. Clementine is BACK. Run.

Joseph Morpurgo: _Work in Progress_

Bits, bobs, odds, sods, fragments, cogs, shards and precious gossamer moments from the Edinburgh Comedy Award nominee and cult character comedian.

Seen/heard in _Austentatious_, _Harry and Paul_, _Drunk History_, Radio 4’s _Joseph Morpurgo’s Walking Tour_, and sundry alt. audiotelevisual scenarios.

May contain adult themes and language.

Please note that line-ups are subject to change.
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Fawlty Towers the Play
description:
The "Greatest British Sitcom of all time" (Radio Times) is back – this time on stage!

Written by John Cleese & Connie Booth

50 years since it first graced our TV screens, Fawlty Towers is now a brand-new stage play adapted by comedy legend John Cleese and directed by Caroline Jay Ranger.

Fresh from a SOLD-OUT West End season! This laugh-out-loud production was universally acclaimed by every London and national critic, selling out every single performance during its record-breaking West-End run.

Join Basil (Danny Bayne), the delightfully bumbling Major (Paul Nicholas) and Polly (Joanne Clifton), with an 18-strong cast as they bring to life all your favourite moments from the show’s unforgettable twelve episodes. With sharp wit, chaos, and calamity at every turn, this is the comedy event of the year!

Secure your tickets now and relive the madness of Fawlty Towers live on stage. It’s a staycation you’ll never forget!

There will be a free touch tour before our audio described matinee performance on Friday 24 July. This event is for blind and partially sighted customers, it provides audio described performance bookers an opportunity to immerse in the world presented on stage by touching parts of the set, props and costumes. Please contact our Box Office to book.
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Edinburgh Comedy Preview
description:
Amy Annette and Andrew White preview their work-in-progress comedy shows ahead of the Edinburgh Fringe.

Get a sneak preview of some of the comedy shows that are about to make a splash at this year’s Edinburgh Fringe.

Amy Annette

This show is about being hot (or not).

As heard on: Off Menu and BBC Radio 4. As seen on: The Way Out (U&Dave). As smelt on: Tour support for Aisling Bea.

“One of my favourite comedians.” (Aisling Bea)

“Brilliant, hilarious. If I didn’t love her so much, I’d hate her.” (Suzi Ruffell)

Andrew White: _What a Life!_

An only-child with two siblings, Andrew White enjoyed a precocious childhood befriending retirees and penning self-indulgent poetry, until a literal circus clown warned him of imminent death on his 18th birthday. All the Jester foretold came to pass, and now Andrew must reflect on life without three close loved ones and do his best to honour their memories. Don’t miss this hilarious, honest and heart-warming new stand-up comedy show.

Andrew has previously toured as support for Catherine Bohart, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Jason Manford.

May contain adult themes and language.

Please note that line-ups are subject to change.
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Mean Girls
description:
Get in loser, we’re going to the Marlowe Theatre!

Winner of Best New Musical (WhatsOnStage Awards), the ferociously funny show Mean Girls is coming to Canterbury direct from the West End. Featuring TV personality, actor and Steps star Faye Tozer, alongside Emily Lane and Vivian Panka.

Mean Girls is brought to the stage by an award-winning creative team including director and choreographer Casey Nicholaw (Disney’s Aladdin and The Book of Mormon), composer Jeff Richmond ( 30 Rock and Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt), lyricist Nell Benjamin ( Legally Blonde), and book writer Tina Fey ( 30 Rock and Bossypants).

Expect all the iconic characters and razor-sharp wit you know and love from the movie, plus some killer songs.

This is so FETCH! Let the Mean Girls reign!

Recommended 10yrs+

Please note this production contains some mature content, haze and strobe lighting.
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: My Heart is Beginning to Break
description:
A new play created with and performed by the Marlowe Theatre Youth Company.

Phoebe’s life is about to implode. Her best friend has announced that they’re moving away and, quite frankly, it’s devastating. This isn’t like any other friendship. It’s special. And the heartbreak is brutal. But Phoebe doesn’t like to talk much about it. Instead, she likes to keep her headphones on, and block things out.

Amidst the angst and literal horror of slowly but surely becoming an adult, is it true that beginnings can sprout from painful endings? And is the rather daring impulse to feel real hope ever truly possible?

Recommended 12yrs+

Please note this production contains mild language, mild references of sex, threat and anxiety.

Learn more about the show with a free post-show talk. No need to book, simply stay in your seats at the end of the performance.

Date: Fri 7 Aug

Time: Post-show
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: The Bodyguard
description:
The smash-hit musical is back and coming to Canterbury.

The award-winning sensation The Bodyguard returns, starring Adam Garcia (Saturday Night Fever, Kiss Me, Kate, Wicked) as Frank Farmer, West End powerhouse Sidonie Smith* (Sister Act, Chicago, Jesus Christ Superstar) as Rachel Marron and Matt Milburn (Coronation Street, Hollyoaks) as Sy Spector.

Former Secret Service agent Frank Farmer is hired to protect global superstar Rachel Marron from an unknown stalker. Both expect to be in charge – neither expects to fall in love.

A “BRILLIANT!” (The Times), breathtakingly romantic thriller, The Bodyguard features a whole host of irresistible classics including One Moment in Time, I Wanna Dance With Somebody and, of course, the legendary I Will Always Love You.

Come and get swept off your feet by this show!

Recommended 10yrs+

*DISCLAIMER: The role of Rachel Marron will be played by Mireia Mambo at selected performances. Cast and performance schedule subject to change without notice. Further casting to be announced.

There will be a free touch tour before our audio described evening performance on Wednesday 26 August. This event is for blind and partially sighted customers, it provides audio described performance bookers an opportunity to immerse in the world presented on stage by touching parts of the set, props and costumes. Please contact our Box Office to book.
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: A Tale of Two Cities
description:
A Tale of Two Cities

It was the best of times. It was the worst of times.

In 1770s Paris, revolution is stirring. Sydney Carton is a brilliant yet cynical barrister from England. Charles Darnay is the rebel child of French aristocrats. When they both fall for charming émigré Lucie Manette, a chain of events is set in motion which will endanger everyone. As the Reign of Terror rages on the streets of Paris, for some, love is a lifeline; for others, a sentence to the guillotine.

Adapted by Terence Rattigan (_The Winslow Boy_, _The Deep Blue Sea_) and John Gielgud, based on one of the best-selling novels of all time, this epic story of love, sacrifice and redemption asks: is there any price too great to pay for love?

_A Tale of Two Cities_ is a Marlowe Theatre production, directed by Michael Fentiman (_Amélie_, RCS’s _Titus Andronicus_, _The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe_, _Loot_) and made in Kent. It premieres here before a national tour.

Recommended 11yrs+

Please note, this production contains references to sexual violence, suicide and the death of a child.

Post-show Talk

Learn more about the show with a free post-show talk. No need to book, simply stay in your seats at the end of the performance.

Date: Fri 18 Sep

Time: Post-show
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Morgan Rees: What A Bummer
description:
The acclaimed stand-up and online sensation is back on the road.

After Morgan and his partner went on separate holidays – one to a Mathematics Forum in Coventry and the other to a Bear Party in Barcelona – a massive revelation forced Morgan to re-evaluate everything. Being banned from Great Western trains, being fired as a Naked Butler, and ghosting Paul McCartney… were they really his fault?

Has that got you hooked? Good, because that’s the only stuff appropriate enough to include in the blurb. A stand-up show about the trials and errors of falling in love and keeping the spark alive, the spiral-inducing limbo of awaiting a diagnosis, and the experience of being a white Welsh valley boy integrating into a proud Gujarati family. Oh, he’s got plenty to talk about.

As well as garnering millions of views online and rave-reviews for his live shows, Morgan has supported Joanne McNally, Rhys James and Jayde Adams on tour, appeared as a finalist in the BBC New Comedy Awards, and hosts hit podcasts My Bad and Chatting With Cherubs alongside Josh Jones and Gearóid Farrelly.

Strictly 18yrs+
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Flo & Joan: With Feeling
description:
The time has come for critically acclaimed musical comedy duo to write another bio as they make their highly anticipated return to the stage with a brand-new show.

“Gosh, they are almost like a real band” (Bruce Dessau, Beyond a Joke). The pair will try and take up as much of the stage and as little of your patience as possible as they use another “real band’s” setlist to write their own show-ruminating on themes such as “what is music?”, “how do you be a band?”, and “is this comedy?” in the sharp-tongued, deadpan way they have become accustomed to.

As seen on Live at the Apollo, The Royal Variety Performance, Comic Relief’s Red Nose Day Live 2023 and their own comedy special Alive on Stage for Amazon Prime.

Recommended 16yrs+

Contains adult themes

Comedy at the Marlowe Theatre is sponsored by Harwood Building Control
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Enter THE CASTLE with Jon Ronson
description:
After three decades investigating human behaviour, Jon’s latest true story turns close to home.

When his son is lured to a mysterious castle deep in the forests of New England, Jon finds himself on a darkly comic, perilous quest to uncover what was really happening inside its walls.

The Castle, Jon’s first book in 11 years, is his most personal, revealing and gripping journey yet: a twisting tale of unmoored men, lost purpose and the absurd, frightening ways it can all go wrong. Does a world of disinhibition, where seemingly anything goes, lie ahead of us?

In this brand-new live show, Jon brings the book’s extraordinary stories to life on stage with astonishing footage and online clips, including jaw-dropping material that has long since vanished from the internet. You’ll also get a first look at what’s coming in the next season of Things Fell Apart, and of course the chance to put your questions directly to Jon. Funny, unsettling, and unforgettable.

Tickets in Price Band P include a copy of The Castle by Jon Ronson – Hardback Edition (RRP £20).

Recommended 14yrs+
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Live at the Marlowe
description:
Nina Conti headlines our unmissable night of live comedy.

Our hugely popular night of live comedy is back, featuring some of the biggest stars of the stand-up circuit.

Headlining is Nina Conti, hot on the heels of the world premiere of her narrative film directorial debut, Sunlight. As seen on QI, The John Bishop Show and Chris O’Dowd’s Family Tree, Nina is an award-winning comedian and ventriloquist.

Our host for the evening is Esther Manito, who began her stand-up career in 2016 while on maternity leave from teaching. In her first year, she gained recognition by being heard on the BBC Radio 4 New Comedy Awards, and has since been seen on Live at the Apollo, ITV’s Stand Up Sketch Show, Hypothetical, and Comedy Central Live.

Also on the bill is Hull-born comedian, actor and writer Amy Gledhill. Winner of Edinburgh Comedy Awards 2024 Best Show, Amy has featured on The Jonathan Ross Show, The One Show, Pointless Celebrities, and The Stand Up Sketch Show.

Completing the line-up is Elis James, a stand-up comedian, actor, and broadcaster best known for his hit BBC Sounds podcast Elis James and John Robins. A regular voice across radio and podcasts, Elis has hosted BBC’s Feast of Football and The Socially Distant Sports Bar, and fronts the comedy–history series Oh What a Time.

Please note that line-ups are subject to change.

May contain adult themes and language.
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Philharmonia Orchestra: Beethoven's Fifth Symphony
description:
One of the most popular pieces in the musical world and beyond.

Delyana Lazarova conductor

Tchaikovsky Romeo and Juliet Fantasy Overture

Grieg Piano Concerto

Beethoven Symphony No. 5

The Philharmonia opens its 2026/27 season at the Marlowe Theatre with one of the most iconic pieces in classical music: Beethoven’s Fifth Symphony.

The electrifying opening of Beethoven’s Symphony No. 5 is one of the most instantly recognisable moments in classical music. But there’s so much more to the piece than that stops-you-in-your-tracks motif. It’s a 40-minute masterclass in orchestral writing that grips you from the first note through to its very end.
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: The Hollow
description:
Agatha Christie’s

The Hollow

A Hercule Poirot mystery.

In a new version by Tamsin Oglesby

Directed by Lucy Bailey

Renowned detective Hercule Poirot expected a quiet weekend break in the country; instead, he’s drawn into one of the most unsettling cases of his career.

At The Hollow, an elegant country estate, a glamorous circle of guests gather together one summer evening. But beneath the sunlit charm, something darker coils. Old passions refuse to die. New rivalries ignite. Dangerous infatuations take hold. Then – a shot rings out.

A body is discovered in the drawing room. A smoking revolver in an unsteady hand. The scene appears almost staged; a shockingly straightforward crime…

It is anything but.

As alibis unravel and masks begin to slip, Poirot finds himself navigating a treacherous web of desire, jealousy and betrayal, where love curdles into cruelty – and everyone has something to hide.

Following sell-out tours of Murder on the Orient Express and Death on the Nile, Fiery Angel reunites with director Lucy Bailey to bring this darkly seductive new staging of one of Agatha Christie’s most compelling mysteries The Hollow to theatres across the UK.

Drama at the Marlowe Theatre is sponsored by Whitehead Monckton.

Learn more about the show with a free post-show talk. No need to book, simply stay in your seats at the end of the performance.

Date: Fri 16 Oct

Time: Post-show
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Michael Akadiri: Don't Call Me Uncle!
description:
Join the “hugely entertaining” (Chortle) Akadiri as he fights a lurid allegation and contemplates the difference between perception and reality, all whilst keeping the laughs flowing.

Following two hit UK tours, Biggest Award in Comedy nominee and resident (junior) Doctor Michael Akadiri prescribes Canterbury with a potent dose of his latest offering, Don’t Call Me Uncle!

Multi-award-winning comedian Michael is proud to wear many hats: doctor, husband & father. Evidently, that list does not include uncle.

Yet, despite only being in his early 30s, “the supremely talented and likeable” (Metro) doctor, for the first time ever, was recently called uncle in public (a term usually reserved for elders!). This has sent him spiralling!

Join the ”‘hugely entertaining” (Chortle) Akadiri as he fights this lurid allegation, contemplates the difference between perception and reality all whilst keeping the laughs flowing with his trademark “sharp-wit” (Chortle) and “politically punchy” (Guardian) anecdotes.

As seen on Comedy Central LIVE, ITVs Stand Up Sketch and Channel 4’s Stand Up to Cancer.

As heard on Capital Xtra’s Breakfast Show, LBC & BBC Radio 4’s Loose Ends tour support for Jason Manford and Emmanuel Sonubi.

Winner – Komedia New Comedy Award

Winner – Comedian of Medicine Award, Haags Comedy Festival

NextUp Biggest Award in Comedy nominee

Comedy at the Marlowe Theatre is sponsored by Harwood Building Control
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Sylvia
description:
Sylvia Pankhurst. Activist. Rebel. Icon.

Torn between loyalty, love, and revolution, see her remarkable story explode onto the stage when Sylvia storms into Canterbury in 2026.

The Olivier Award winning electrifying musical is fueled by an irresistible soundtrack that joyously unites hip hop, funk and soul, by Josh Cohen and DJ Walde and brought to life by world-renowned choreographer and storyteller Kate Prince ( ZooNation).

Powerhouse talent Beverley Knight ( Sister Act, The Drifters Girl, Memphis the Musical) will reprise her Olivier award winning role as Emmeline Pankhurst.

Book now for this revolutionary, uplifting musical direct from London’s Old Vic theatre.

Recommended 10yrs+

Please note this performance contains flashing lights
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: The All-New Adventures of Peter Pan
description:
Flying to the Marlowe Theatre in 2026: HOOK your tickets for a magical family pantomime adventure.

The show will of course star Marlowe Legend Ben Roddy (winner of Best Dame at the Pantomime Awards 2024), alongside an all-star cast to be announced… stay tuned!

Once again, this Pan-to will be written and directed by Paul Hendy, (winner of Best Script at the Pantomime Awards 2024), and produced by the Marlowe Theatre and Evolution Productions, the same award-winning team that brought you the box office smash-hits _Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs_ and _Beauty and the Beast_.

It’s never too early to book, with lavish sets, hilarious jokes, fabulous music, it’s fun for the whole family! HOOK your tickets now!
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: The Reluctant Dragon
description:
A brand-new family musical about friendship, unlikely heroes, and having the courage to be different.

When a fairytale-obsessed child discovers a real-life dragon living in a nearby cave, panic spreads throughout the village. But instead of a fire-breathing monster, the child gets to know a friendly and flamboyant creature who loves to perform poetry and bask in the sunshine.

To save the dragon from St. George the Dragon-Slayer, the new friends must mastermind a cunning plan. Can a charming dragon, a noble knight and one clever child manage to trick the village and prove that not all dragons are monsters?

This original Marlowe Theatre production is an enchanting adaptation of the beloved story by Kenneth Grahame ( _The Wind in the Willows_), full of laughter, music and heart. With book and lyrics by Samson Hawkins ( _Village Idiot_), music by Joey Hickman ( _The Snow Queen_) and direction by Lucy Wray ( _Fertile_, _Celebrate_; RSC Associate Learning Practitioner), _The Reluctant Dragon_ is the perfect introduction to theatre for young children this festive season.

Recommended 3yrs+
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Matthew Bourne's Cinderella
description:
A heart-stopping depiction of the familiar but timeless fable set against the backdrop of the Second World War.

New Adventures return with the 30th Anniversary celebration of one of their most popular and beloved productions, Matthew Bourne’s Cinderella – a thrilling and evocative love story, set in London during the Second World War.

Matthew Bourne’s epic interpretation of the classic fairytale has, at its heart, a true wartime romance. A chance meeting results in a magical night for Cinderella and her dashing young RAF pilot, together just long enough to fall in love before being parted by the horrors of the Blitz.

With Lez Brotherston’s glorious Olivier Award-winning designs, lighting by Olivier Award-winning Neil Austin, and video and projection designs by Duncan McLean, Cinderella features sensational sound design by Paul Groothuis. This production will take the audience into the heart of Prokofiev’s magnificent score and the sights and sounds of war-torn London.

Recommended 8yrs+

Please be advised that this production is set in London during the Blitz of 1940. It contains depictions of bombing raids, destruction of buildings, sirens, and explosions through the use of theatrical smoke/haze, strobe lighting, and loud noises.

Ahead of the matinee performance on Saturday 27 March commencing there will be a short curtain raiser performance by the New Adventure’s Cygnets.

Learn more about the show with a free post-show talk. No need to book, simply stay in your seats at the end of the performance. This talk will be BSL interpreted.

Date Wed 24 Mar

Time Post-show
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Ed Gamble: Fresh Hell
description:
Everyone’s annoying cousin, Ed Gamble, is going back on tour in 2027 across the UK and Ireland with some more of his stand-up grot.

Come and see the self-appointed Crown Prince of Visualised Companion Podcasts harrumph about the stage. Co-host of the award-winning mega-hit podcast Off Menu with James Acaster, host of visualised spin-offs The Traitors: Uncloaked and Taskmaster the Podcast, as well as new upcoming panel show Unacceptableairing this summer, Ed’s brand-new tour, Fresh Hell, follows his biggest ever sold-out world tour across Europe, North America and Australasia.

Recommended 14yrs+
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS

title: Mrs. Doubtfire
description:
Help is on the way, Dear!

After dazzling audiences in the West End, Mrs. Doubtfire is heading to the Marlowe Theatre! With fabulous new songs, dazzling sets and incredible dance numbers, this “genuinely funny” (Time Out) show is not to be missed.

Out-of-work actor Daniel will do anything for his kids. After losing custody in a messy divorce, he creates the alter ego of Scottish nanny Euphegenia Doubtfire in a desperate attempt to stay in their lives. As his new character takes on a life of its own, Mrs. Doubtfire teaches Daniel more than he bargained for about how to be a father.

Book now to avoid missing out on this “surefire hit” (The Telegraph, Daily Express).

Recommended 6yrs+
address: Marlowe Theatre, The Friars, Canterbury, CT1 2AS
